div1.entryFree:
Unfelt, (unfélt or únfelt, according as the following subst. is accentuated: unfélt imáginátion; únfelt sórrow etc.) 1) not felt, not affecting the heart: for u. imagination they often feel a world of restless cares, R3 I, 4, 80. to show an u. sorrow is an office which the false man does easy, Mcb. II, 3, 142.
2) not perceived, not affecting the senses: O unseen shame, invisible disgrace, O u. sore, crest-wounding private scar, Lucr. 828. all my treasury is yet but u. thanks, R2 II, 3, 61 (perhaps with intentional ambiguity).
